project: Ramayana PS5 — ELGODS Kishkindha Kanda moment corpus
designPillars: 10 Kishkindha Kanda moments derived from ELGODS source citations
Valmiki sarga citations preserved per moment
Voice cue IDs follow Day 13 register pattern vc_kishkindha_<verb>_<id>

moments.narrative: When I see Rama, something breaks open inside me. I am Hanuman, son of Vayu. I am yours. I leap from the tree and land at his feet. Sugriva watches. The future of Lanka trembles.
moments.moralLesson: Recognition of the divine can come in a single glance

moments.narrative: Hanuman, remember your powers! You are the son of Vayu, the wind god. You are the eleventh avatar of Rudra. Leap, Vanara. The ocean cannot hold you. Lanka cannot hide from you.
moments.moralLesson: A mentor's faith can unlock strength you forgot you had

moments.narrative: Hanuman grows to the size of a mountain. He crouches on the mountain peak and launches himself across the ocean. The waves bow. The fish look up. Lanka sees his shadow and trembles.
moments.moralLesson: One leap of faith can bridge an ocean

moments.narrative: The Ashoka grove. I climb a flowering tree and look down. Sita sits in the center, surrounded by rakshasas. Her hair is unbound. Her face is a moon behind clouds. I drop a ring. She looks up.
moments.moralLesson: Hope survives even in the darkest grove

moments.narrative: I find her under the Ashoka tree. Mother Sita, Rama sent me. I am his messenger. She does not believe — until I give her the ring. The ring he gave her at their wedding. Recognition breaks like dawn.
moments.moralLesson: A single token of love carries more truth than a thousand words

moments.narrative: Rama is alive. He is coming. He sent this ring as proof. But will he come? Or has fourteen years broken his love? Hanuman bows. I will carry you on my back if I have to. She laughs — the first laugh in Lanka.
moments.moralLesson: Hope needs no proof but trust

moments.narrative: They set his tail on fire. Hanuman leaps rooftop to rooftop, setting Lanka ablaze. Not out of rage — out of necessity. The city of Lanka teaches respect for one vanara's tail.
moments.moralLesson: Even destruction can be an act of justice

moments.narrative: Lakshmana falls in the war. I fly to the Himalayas and lift the entire mountain — the mountain of herbs — and carry it back. The rishi is surprised. The mountain is surprised. Lanka sees the shadow of a flying peak.
moments.moralLesson: Love can lift what war has felled

moments.narrative: Rama meets Sugriva in the Rishyamooka hills. Two exiles. Two crowns lost. One promise made: I will help you regain your kingdom; you will help me find Sita. The alliance is sealed not by oath but by fire.
moments.moralLesson: Alliance forged in shared exile can topple a king

moments.narrative: Sugriva is crowned king of Kishkindha. Rama stands beside him. The vanara army is assembled. The southern ocean waits. Hanuman waits. The road to Lanka is open.
moments.moralLesson: A just king crowned with allies is unstoppable
